ops.title: webnav spike: PageIndex-style outline enrichment rejected
ops.body: 180 isolated haiku trials (6 docs x 6 gold questions x 5 arms) on branch spike/webnav: shipped hybrid web search is the most accurate AND cheapest navigator (92%, $0.029/trial median); extractive digests score BELOW plain headings on structured docs (88% vs 96%); sonnet per-node summaries only tie plain headings at +14% trial cost + 1.2M tokens indexing. Verdict: no outline enrichment, no web annotate op, no plugin indexer agent. Four measured product gaps to fix instead: (1) raw-.md URLs mangled by Extract (HTML-escaping kills structure), (2) keyless PDFs stored as %PDF bytes by the http tier, (3) web read rejects printed section numbers with a bare 'section not found', (4) degenerate single-section outlines make section reads head-of-page only. Memo: docs/webnav-spike.md on spike/webnav (not on main).
